content.text:
Here's a clear picture of the current state and what's needed for mobile compatibility testing:

## Current E2E Test Setup

The existing e2e tests (`tests/e2e/`) use plain `fetch()` calls — they check HTTP status and HTML content but have **no browser automation, no viewport testing, and no visual rendering**. They can't detect mobile layout issues.

## Recommended Approach: Playwright

The best fit for mobile compatibility testing here is **Playwright**, which supports:
- Built-in device emulation (exact viewport + user-agent for real devices)
- Visual screenshot comparison
- Touch event simulation
- No real device required

Playwright is already present in `node_modules` (via Next.js's `experimental/testmode`), but not set up as a test runner.

## Proposed Coverage

**Devices to emulate** (Playwright has built-in profiles for most of these):

| Brand | Models | Width |
|---|---|---|
| Apple | iPhone SE | 375px |
| Apple | iPhone 14 Pro / 15 Pro | 393px |
| Apple | iPhone 14 Pro Max / 15 Plus | 430px |
| Apple | iPad Mini | 744px |
| Apple | iPad Pro 12.9" | 1024px |
| Google | Pixel 5 / 7 | 393px |
| Samsung | Galaxy S21 / S23 | 360px |
| Generic Android | 360–412px range | — |

**What to test on each:**

1. **Layout integrity** — no horizontal overflow (`document.body.scrollWidth <= window.innerWidth`)
2. **Navigation** — hamburger/mobile menu renders and is tappable
3. **Font sizes** — body text >= 16px (prevents iOS auto-zoom on inputs)
4. **Touch targets** — buttons/links >= 44×44px (Apple HIG minimum)
5. **Images** — scale within container, no overflow
6. **Code blocks / tables** — horizontal scroll within container, not full page
7. **Search modal** — opens correctly, keyboard doesn't push layout
8. **TOC** — hidden/collapsed on mobile, accessible
9. **Series sidebar** — collapsed or converted to dropdown on small screens
10. **Reading progress bar** — renders correctly

## Implementation Plan

1. Install `@playwright/test` + browser binaries
2. Add `playwright.config.ts` with device matrix
3. Create `tests/e2e/mobile.spec.ts` with the above checks
4. Add `test:mobile` script to `package.json`

---

Want me to go ahead and implement this? I can start with a focused set of devices (e.g., iPhone SE, iPhone Pro Max, Pixel 5, Galaxy S21, iPad) and the most critical checks (overflow, navigation, touch targets), which gives broad coverage without excessive test runtime.

{
  "name": "@hutusi/amytis",
  "version": "1.11.0",
  "description": "A high-performance digital garden and blog engine with Next.js 16 and Tailwind CSS v4",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "git+https://github.com/hutusi/amytis.git"
  },
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/hutusi/amytis/issues"
  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/hutusi/amytis#readme",
  "private": false,
  "packageManager": "bun@1.3.4",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",
    "build": "bun scripts/copy-assets.ts && bun run build:graph && next build && next-image-export-optimizer && pagefind --site out",
    "build:dev": "bun scripts/copy-assets.ts && bun run build:graph && next build && pagefind --site out --output-path public/pagefind",
    "build:graph": "NODE_ENV=production bun scripts/generate-knowledge-graph.ts",
    "validate": "bun run lint && bun run test && bun run build:dev",
    "clean": "rm -rf .next out public/posts public/books public/flows",
    "new": "bun scripts/new-post.ts",
    "new-weekly": "bun scripts/new-post.ts --series ai-nexus-weekly --md --folder --prefix weekly",
    "new-series": "bun scripts/new-series.ts",
    "new-from-pdf": "bun scripts/new-from-pdf.ts",
    "new-from-images": "bun scripts/new-from-images.ts",
    "new-flow": "bun scripts/new-flow.ts",
    "new-flow-from-chat": "bun scripts/new-flow-from-chat.ts",
    "new-note": "bun scripts/new-note.ts",
    "import-book": "bun scripts/import-book.ts",
    "sync-book": "bun scripts/sync-book-chapters.ts",
    "series-draft": "bun scripts/series-draft.ts",
    "deploy": "bun scripts/deploy.ts",
    "start": "next start",
    "lint": "eslint",
    "test": "bun test src tests/unit tests/tooling packages/create-amytis/src && bun run test:int",
    "test:unit": "bun test src tests/unit packages/create-amytis/src",
    "test:int": "bun test tests/integration",
    "test:e2e": "bun test tests/e2e"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@giscus/react": "^3.1.0",
    "@tailwindcss/typography": "^0.5.19",
    "d3": "^7.9.0",
    "github-slugger": "^2.0.0",
    "gray-matter": "^4.0.3",
    "image-size": "^2.0.2",
    "katex": "^0.16.33",
    "mermaid": "^11.12.3",
    "next": "16.1.6",
    "next-image-export-optimizer": "^1.20.1",
    "next-themes": "^0.4.6",
    "react": "19.2.4",
    "react-dom": "19.2.4",
    "react-icons": "^5.5.0",
    "react-markdown": "^10.1.0",
    "react-syntax-highlighter": "^16.1.0",
    "rehype-katex": "^7.0.1",
    "rehype-raw": "^7.0.0",
    "rehype-slug": "^6.0.0",
    "rehype-stringify": "^10.0.1",
    "remark-gfm": "^4.0.1",
    "remark-math": "^6.0.0",
    "remark-parse": "^11.0.0",
    "remark-rehype": "^11.1.2",
    "unified": "^11.0.5",
    "unist-util-visit": "^5.1.0",
    "zod": "^4.3.6"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@playwright/test": "^1.58.2",
    "@tailwindcss/postcss": "^4.1.18",
    "@types/bun": "^1.3.9",
    "@types/d3": "^7.4.3",
    "@types/hast": "^3.0.4",
    "@types/image-size": "^0.8.0",
    "@types/mdast": "^4.0.4",
    "@types/node": "^24.10.13",
    "@types/react": "^19.2.14",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.2.3",
    "@types/react-syntax-highlighter": "^15.5.13",
    "babel-plugin-react-compiler": "1.0.0",
    "eslint": "^9.0.0",
    "eslint-config-next": "16.1.6",
    "pagefind": "^1.4.0",
    "pdf-to-img": "^5.0.0",
    "tailwindcss": "^4.1.18",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3"
  },
  "ignoreScripts": [
    "sharp",
    "unrs-resolver"
  ],
  "trustedDependencies": [
    "sharp",
    "unrs-resolver"
  ],
  "workspaces": ["packages/*"]
}

Clean. Here's a summary of everything created:

---

## What was set up

### Files created/modified

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `playwright.config.ts` | Playwright config with 17 device profiles |
| `tests/e2e/mobile/mobile-compat.spec.ts` | 22 tests × 17 devices = **374 total tests** |
| `package.json` | Added `"test:mobile": "playwright test"` |

### Device coverage

**Apple (WebKit):** iPhone SE · iPhone 14 Pro · iPhone 14 Pro Max · iPad Mini · iPad Pro 11

**Google (Chromium):** Pixel 5 · Pixel 7

**Samsung (Chromium):** Galaxy S8 · Galaxy S21

**Huawei (Chromium):** P50 Pro · Mate 60

**Xiaomi (Chromium):** Xiaomi 14 · Redmi Note 13

**Oppo (Chromium):** Find X7 · Reno 11

**Vivo (Chromium):** X100 · Y100

### Tests per device

1. **Viewport meta** — `width=device-width, initial-scale=1` present
2. **No horizontal overflow** — 6 pages checked (`/`, `/posts`, `/series`, `/tags`, `/archive`, post page)
3. **Navigation variant** — hamburger on phones (<768px), desktop nav on tablets (≥768px)
4. **Mobile menu open/close** — tap to open, × to close
5. **Menu closes on link tap** — navigates and dismisses
6. **Touch targets** — hamburger ≥ 40×40px, theme toggle ≥ 32px
7. **Post sidebar hidden** on viewports < 1024px
8. **HorizontalScroll arrows hidden** on phones
9. **Body font size** ≥ 14px
10. **Images don't overflow** their containers (homepage + post page)
11. **Scroll lock** — `body.style.overflow=hidden` when menu open, cleared on close
12. **Tags page overflow** check
13. **Archive page overflow** check

### Usage

```bash
# Requires dev server running (auto-starts if not)
bun run test:mobile

# Run just one device
bunx playwright test --project="iPhone SE"

# Run with headed browser (visual)
bunx playwright test --headed --project="iPhone 14 Pro"

# Open HTML report after run
bunx playwright show-report
```

content.text:
Now I have everything. Here's my analysis:

---

## Results: 17 failed, 345 passed, 12 skipped

### Failure 1 — Hamburger touch target (15 devices, all phones)

**The actual error:**
```
Expected: >= 40
Received:    36
```

The hamburger button in `Navbar.tsx:287` has `p-2` (8px padding) around a `20×20` SVG:
```
20px (icon) + 8px + 8px padding = 36px total
```

This is a **real UX bug**. Apple HIG minimum is 44×44px, Material Design is 48×48px. 36×36px is too small to tap reliably, especially on smaller phones (iPhone SE, Galaxy S8 360px). The fix is increasing the button padding from `p-2` to `p-3` (12px each side → 44px total).

---

### Failure 2 — Image overflow on post page (Pixel 7, 1 occurrence)

**Passed on rerun** — this was a **flaky test**. The `kitchen-sink` post has Mermaid diagrams, syntax highlighting, and KaTeX that load asynchronously. `waitForLoadState('networkidle')` wasn't enough; an image dimensions check ran before lazy-loaded images settled. The test needs a more robust wait strategy.

---

### Failure 3 — SeriesList visible on mobile post page (Pixel 7, 1 occurrence)

Also **passed on rerun** — another **flaky test** for the same reason (the post page content hadn't fully rendered when the assertion ran).

---

| # | Issue | Type | Fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Hamburger button 36px < 44px minimum | **Real UX bug** | Increase `p-2` → `p-3` in Navbar |
| 2 | Image overflow test flaky on post page | **Test timing issue** | Wait for images to load before asserting |
| 3 | SeriesList test flaky on post page | **Test timing issue** | Same — add explicit content ready wait |

- Main article: `min-w-0 w-full max-w-3xl mx-auto overflow-x-hidden`
- Post cards: `flex flex-col h-full`

I can use `page.locator('article.min-w-0')` to target the main article specifically, or grab the first article on the page with `.first()`. The intent for the image overflow test is to wait for the main article to be visible, so I'll use a selector that uniquely identifies it.

The cleanest approach is `page.locator('article').first()` since it avoids strict mode violations and reliably targets the main post content. I need to apply this fix to both places where the article locator is used with `.waitFor()`.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

Amytis is a static "digital garden" blog built with Next.js 15+ (App Router), React 19, and Tailwind CSS v4. Content is authored in MDX/Markdown files and statically generated at build time. Features include series support, multi-language (i18n), configurable themes, and comments integration.

## Commands

```bash
# Development
bun dev                    # Start dev server at localhost:3000
bun run lint               # Run ESLint

# Testing
bun test                   # Run all tests
bun run test:unit          # Run unit tests (src/)
bun run test:int           # Run integration tests
bun run test:e2e           # Run end-to-end tests
bun test path/to/file.test.ts  # Run a single test file

# Build
bun run build              # Full production build (copies assets, builds Next.js, optimizes images)
bun run build:dev          # Development build (no image optimization, faster) — also regenerates Pagefind search index in public/pagefind/
bun run clean              # Remove .next, out, public/posts directories

# Deploy
bun run deploy             # Deploy out/ to Linux/nginx server via rsync+sshpass (reads .env.local)

# Content creation
bun run new "Post Title"              # Create new post
bun run new-series "Series Name"      # Create new series
bun run new-from-pdf doc.pdf          # Create post from PDF
bun run new-from-images ./photos      # Create post from image folder
bun run new-flow                      # Create today's flow note
bun run new-flow-from-chat            # Import all new files from imports/chats/
bun run sync-book                     # Sync chapters list for all books from disk
bun run sync-book <slug>              # Sync chapters list for one book
```

## Architecture

### Data Flow

1. **Content source**: MDX/Markdown files in `content/posts/` and `content/series/`
2. **Data layer**: `src/lib/markdown.ts` - reads files with Node `fs`, parses frontmatter with `gray-matter`, validates with Zod
3. **Static generation**: Routes use `generateStaticParams` to pre-render at build time
4. **Rendering**: `react-markdown` with remark/rehype plugins for GFM, math (KaTeX), syntax highlighting, and Mermaid diagrams

   104→bun run build              # Production build (includes image optimization and Pagefind index)
   105→bun run build:dev          # Development build (faster, no image optimization; also generates Pagefind index)
   106→```
   107→
   108→## Static Export Routing Rules
   109→
   110→Amytis relies on static export (`output: "export"`) with `trailingSlash: true`.
   111→
   112→- In `generateStaticParams()`, return raw segment values and let Next.js handle encoding.
   113→- Do not pre-encode route params with `encodeURIComponent`.
   114→- Do not link to placeholder routes such as `/posts/[slug]`; always link to concrete URLs.
   115→- When touching dynamic routes, verify both ASCII and Unicode slugs.
   116→
   117→## Code Style
   118→
   119→- **Linting:** `bun run lint`
   120→- **TypeScript:** Use strict types.
   121→- **Tailwind:** Use utility classes.
   122→- **Localization:** Use `t()` helper for UI strings.

# Contributing to Amytis

## Getting Started

1. **Install Bun:** Ensure you have [Bun](https://bun.sh/) (1.3.4+) installed.
2. **Install Dependencies:**
   ```bash
   bun install
   ```
3. **Run Development Server:**
   ```bash
   bun dev
   ```
   Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000).

   > **Search in dev:** The search index is generated by `pagefind` at build time. Run `bun run build:dev` once to generate `public/pagefind/`. After that, the search modal (Cmd/Ctrl+K) will work while `bun dev` is running. Re-run `bun run build:dev` whenever content changes.

## Writing Content

### Creating Posts

Use the CLI to scaffold new content:

```bash

bun run build:dev          # Development build (faster, no image optimization; also generates Pagefind index)
```

## Static Export Routing Rules

Amytis relies on static export (`output: "export"`) with `trailingSlash: true`.

- In `generateStaticParams()`, return raw segment values and let Next.js handle encoding.
- Do not pre-encode route params with `encodeURIComponent`.
- Do not link to placeholder routes such as `/posts/[slug]`; always link to concrete URLs.
- When touching dynamic routes, verify both ASCII and Unicode slugs.

## Code Style

- **Linting:** `bun run lint`
- **TypeScript:** Use strict types.
- **Tailwind:** Use utility classes.
- **Localization:** Use `t()` helper for UI strings.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

Amytis is a static "digital garden" blog built with Next.js 15+ (App Router), React 19, and Tailwind CSS v4. Content is authored in MDX/Markdown files and statically generated at build time. Features include series support, multi-language (i18n), configurable themes, and comments integration.

## Commands

```bash
# Development
bun dev                    # Start dev server at localhost:3000
bun run lint               # Run ESLint

# Testing
bun test                   # Run all tests
bun run test:unit          # Run unit tests (src/)
bun run test:int           # Run integration tests
bun run test:e2e           # Run end-to-end tests
bun test path/to/file.test.ts  # Run a single test file

# Build
bun run build              # Full production build (copies assets, builds Next.js, optimizes images)
bun run build:dev          # Development build (no image optimization, faster) — also regenerates Pagefind search index in public/pagefind/
bun run clean              # Remove .next, out, public/posts directories

# Deploy
bun run deploy             # Deploy out/ to Linux/nginx server via rsync+sshpass (reads .env.local)

# Content creation
bun run new "Post Title"              # Create new post
bun run new-series "Series Name"      # Create new series
bun run new-from-pdf doc.pdf          # Create post from PDF
bun run new-from-images ./photos      # Create post from image folder
bun run new-flow                      # Create today's flow note
bun run new-flow-from-chat            # Import all new files from imports/chats/
bun run sync-book                     # Sync chapters list for all books from disk
bun run sync-book <slug>              # Sync chapters list for one book
```

## Architecture

### Data Flow

1. **Content source**: MDX/Markdown files in `content/posts/` and `content/series/`
2. **Data layer**: `src/lib/markdown.ts` - reads files with Node `fs`, parses frontmatter with `gray-matter`, validates with Zod
3. **Static generation**: Routes use `generateStaticParams` to pre-render at build time
4. **Rendering**: `react-markdown` with remark/rehype plugins for GFM, math (KaTeX), syntax highlighting, and Mermaid diagrams

### Key Files

- `site.config.ts` - Site configuration (nav, social, pagination, themes, i18n, analytics, comments)
- `src/lib/markdown.ts` - Data access layer with all content query functions
- `src/lib/urls.ts` - Central URL helpers (`getPostUrl`, `getPostsBasePath`, `getSeriesCustomPaths`, etc.) — always use these instead of hardcoding `/posts/[slug]`
- `src/lib/search-utils.ts` - Pure search utilities (URL type detection, date extraction, title cleaning, markdown stripping) shared by `Search` and the search index route
- `src/app/globals.css` - Theme CSS variables and color palettes
- `src/components/MarkdownRenderer.tsx` - MDX rendering with all plugins
- `src/i18n/translations.ts` - Language strings for i18n

### Route Structure

- `/` - Homepage with hero, featured series, featured posts, latest writing
- `/posts/[slug]` - Individual post pages
- `/posts/page/[page]` - Paginated post listing
- `/series` - All series overview
- `/series/[slug]` - Single series with its posts
- `/series/[slug]/page/[page]` - Series pagination
- `/tags` - Tag cloud with post counts
- `/tags/[tag]` - Posts filtered by tag
- `/authors/[author]` - Posts filtered by author
- `/archive` - Chronological listing grouped by year/month
- `/books` - All books overview
- `/books/[slug]` - Single book with chapter listing
- `/books/[slug]/[chapter]` - Individual chapter page
- `/flows` - Flow notes listing (paginated)
- `/flows/page/[page]` - Paginated flow listing
- `/flows/[year]` - Flows filtered by year
- `/flows/[year]/[month]` - Flows filtered by month
- `/flows/[year]/[month]/[day]` - Single flow detail page
- `/[slug]` - Static pages (about, subscribe, etc.) and custom posts/series listing pages
- `/[prefix]/[slug]` - Posts at custom URL prefixes (e.g. `/articles/my-post`, `/weeklies/my-post`)
- `/[prefix]/page/[page]` - Paginated listing at custom URL prefixes

### Content Structure

**Posts** support two formats:
- **Flat file**: `content/posts/my-post.mdx`
- **Nested folder**: `content/posts/my-post/index.mdx` (allows co-located images in `./images/`)

**Series** live in `content/series/[slug]/index.mdx` with optional `images/` folder for cover images.

**Books** live in `content/books/[slug]/` with `index.mdx` for metadata and separate `.mdx` files per chapter. The `index.mdx` frontmatter defines chapter ordering with an optional parts structure.

**Flows** (daily notes) live in `content/flows/YYYY/MM/DD.md` (or `.mdx`). Each flow has a date, title, excerpt, tags, and markdown content.

Date-prefixed filenames (`2026-01-01-my-post.mdx`) extract dates automatically.

## Config Files

There are two config files that must be kept in sync:

- **`site.config.ts`** — the live config for this repo (i18n enabled; locale-aware fields use `{ en: '...', zh: '...' }` objects)
- **`site.config.example.ts`** — single-language starter template shipped via `create-amytis`; locale-aware fields use plain strings; optional features (books, flow) default to disabled

**Rule:** Any schema change to `site.config.ts` (new field, renamed field, type change) must be mirrored in `site.config.example.ts`. Locale-aware values (`string | Record<string, string>`) should use plain strings in the example.

## Configuration (`site.config.ts`)

Key configuration options:
- `nav` - Navigation links with weights
- `social` - GitHub, Twitter, email links for footer
- `series.navbar` - Series slugs to show in navbar dropdown
- `series.customPaths` - Per-series custom URL prefix e.g. `{ 'weeklies': 'weeklies' }` → posts at `/weeklies/[slug]`
- `pagination.posts`, `pagination.series` - Items per page
- `themeColor` - 'default' | 'blue' | 'rose' | 'amber'
- `hero` - Homepage hero title and subtitle
- `i18n` - Default locale and supported locales
- `featured.series` - Scrollable series: `scrollThreshold` (default: 2), `maxItems` (default: 6)
- `featured.stories` - Scrollable stories: `scrollThreshold` (default: 1), `maxItems` (default: 5)
- `analytics.providers` - array of enabled providers: `['umami', 'google']`; `[]` disables analytics
- `comments.provider` - 'giscus' | 'disqus' | null
- `feed.format` - 'rss' | 'atom' | 'both'
- `feed.content` - 'full' | 'excerpt'
- `feed.maxItems` - max feed items (0 = unlimited)
- `footer.bottomLinks` - custom links in the footer bottom bar (ICP, cookie policy, etc.); `text` accepts plain string or `{ en, zh }` locale map
- `posts.basePath` - Custom URL prefix for all posts (default: `'posts'`); e.g. `'articles'` → posts at `/articles/[slug]`
- `posts.authors.default` - Fallback authors when a post has none set in frontmatter
- `posts.authors.showInHeader` - Show author byline below post title (default: true)
- `posts.authors.showAuthorCard` - Show author card at end of post (default: true)
- `posts.excludeFromListing` - Series slugs whose posts are hidden from `/posts` listing pages
- `authors` - Per-author profiles: `bio`, `avatar` (image path), `social` (array of `{ image, description }`)

## Content Frontmatter

### Posts

```yaml
---
title: "Post Title"
subtitle: "Optional subtitle line"  # Rendered below the title in italic
date: "2026-01-01"
excerpt: "Optional summary (auto-generated if omitted)"
category: "Category Name"
tags: ["tag1", "tag2"]
authors: ["Author Name"]
series: "series-slug"      # Link to a series
draft: true                # Hidden in production
featured: true             # Show in featured section
pinned: true               # Always shown in featured section; never shuffled away (hero = most recent pinned)
coverImage: "./images/cover.jpg"  # Local or external URL
latex: true                # Enable KaTeX math
toc: false                 # Hide table of contents
layout: "simple"           # Use simple layout (default: "post")
externalLinks:             # Links to external discussions
  - name: "Hacker News"
    url: "https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=12345"
  - name: "V2EX"
    url: "https://v2ex.com/t/123456"
---
```

### Series (`content/series/[slug]/index.mdx`)

```yaml
---
title: "Series Title"
excerpt: "Series description"
date: "2026-01-01"
coverImage: "./images/cover.jpg"
featured: true             # Show in featured series
draft: true                # Hidden in production (default: false)
sort: "date-asc"           # 'date-asc' | 'date-desc' | 'manual'
posts: ["post-1", "post-2"] # Manual post ordering (optional)
---
```

### Books (`content/books/[slug]/index.mdx`)

```yaml
---
title: "Book Title"
excerpt: "Book description"
date: "2026-01-01"
coverImage: "text:DG"           # Cover image or text placeholder
featured: true                  # Show in featured books
draft: false
authors: ["Author Name"]
chapters:
  - part: "Part I: Getting Started"    # Optional part grouping
    chapters:
      - title: "Chapter Title"
        id: "chapter-file"             # Maps to chapter-file.mdx or chapter-file/index.mdx
  - part: "Part II: Advanced"
    chapters:
      - title: "Another Chapter"
        id: "another-chapter"
---
```

## Key Components

- `PostLayout` / `SimpleLayout` - Post page layouts with TOC, series sidebar, external links, comments
- `Hero` - Configurable homepage hero section with collapsible intro
- `HorizontalScroll` - Scrollable container with navigation arrows for featured content
- `PostList` - Card-based post listing with thumbnails, metadata, excerpts, and tags
- `SeriesCatalog` - Timeline-style series post listing with numbered entries and progress indicator
- `SeriesSidebar` - Series navigation sidebar with progress bar and color-coded states
- `SeriesList` - Mobile-optimized series navigation matching sidebar design
- `Search` - Full-text search (Cmd/Ctrl+K) powered by Pagefind (build-time index); features type filter tabs (All/Post/Flow/Book), recent searches, keyboard navigation (arrows + number keys), debounced input, body scroll lock, focus trap, ARIA accessibility, and search syntax tips (`"phrase"`, `-exclude`)
- `TableOfContents` - Sticky TOC with scroll tracking, reading progress, and back-to-top
- `MarkdownRenderer` - MDX rendering with GFM, math, syntax highlighting, diagrams
- `CoverImage` - Optimized image component with WebP support
- `Comments` - Giscus or Disqus integration (theme-aware)
- `Analytics` - Umami, Plausible, or Google Analytics integration
- `FlowContent` - Client wrapper for flow pages with tag filtering state management
- `FlowCalendarSidebar` - Calendar sidebar with date navigation, browse panel, and clickable tag filters
- `FlowTimelineEntry` - Individual flow entry in timeline list
- `LanguageSwitch` - i18n language selector
- `ThemeToggle` - Light/dark mode toggle

### Mobile Compatibility Tests

`bun run test:mobile` uses [Playwright](https://playwright.dev/) to test the site across 17 real-device profiles:

- **Apple:** iPhone SE, iPhone 14 Pro, iPhone 14 Pro Max, iPad Mini, iPad Pro 11
- **Google:** Pixel 5, Pixel 7
- **Samsung:** Galaxy S8, Galaxy S21
- **Huawei:** P50 Pro, Mate 60
- **Xiaomi:** Xiaomi 14, Redmi Note 13
- **Oppo:** Find X7, Reno 11
- **Vivo:** X100, Y100

**First-time setup** — install the browser binaries once:

```bash
bunx playwright install chromium webkit
```

The test server starts automatically (`bun dev`) if one is not already running. Useful commands:

```bash
bunx playwright test --project="iPhone SE"          # Run one device
bunx playwright test --project="iPhone SE" --headed # Headed (visible) browser
bunx playwright show-report                         # Open HTML report
```

Tests cover: no horizontal overflow, navigation (hamburger on phones, desktop nav on tablets), mobile menu open/close, touch target sizing (≥44px), post sidebar visibility, scroll lock, font sizes, and image overflow.

# Contributing to Amytis

Create a git commit for the current changes. Follow these steps:

1. Run these commands in parallel to understand the current state:
   - `git status` (never use -uall flag)
   - `git diff` to see both staged and unstaged changes
   - `git log --oneline -5` to see recent commit message style

2. Analyze all changes and draft a commit message:
   - Use conventional commit format: `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `refactor:`, `test:`, `chore:`, etc.
   - Keep the first line under 72 characters, focusing on the "why" not the "what"
   - Add a blank line and details in the body if the change is non-trivial
   - Do not commit files that likely contain secrets (.env, credentials, etc.)

3. Stage relevant files by name (prefer specific files over `git add -A`) and create the commit:
   - End the commit message with: `Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>`
   - Use a HEREDOC for the message to ensure correct formatting
   - Run `git status` after the commit to verify success

4. If commit fails due to pre-commit hooks, fix the issue and create a NEW commit (never amend).

5. Do NOT push to remote unless explicitly asked.

If there are no changes to commit, inform the user and do nothing.

The user is asking about HarmonyOS Next coverage in the mobile tests. This is a good question. Let me think about this:

**HarmonyOS Next** is Huawei's latest OS (launched 2024), which is a complete departure from Android. Unlike previous HarmonyOS versions (which were AOSP-based), HarmonyOS Next:
- Has its own kernel (not Linux/Android)
- Uses its own browser engine based on WebKit (not Chromium/Blink)
- The default browser is **Huawei Browser** which uses a fork of WebKit
- Comes with Kirin chips (Huawei's own SoC)


- Targets devices like the Mate 60 series and Pura 70 series

The problem is that our current Huawei test profiles are outdated—they're configured for Chromium with Android user-agents, but the Mate 60 actually ships with HarmonyOS Next and its WebKit-based Huawei Browser, while the older P50 Pro still runs the Android-based EMUI. So we're not properly testing against the actual browser engines these devices use.

I should create a new HarmonyOS Next profile that uses WebKit and the correct user-agent string for that OS, with the appropriate viewport dimensions for devices like the Mate 60 Pro. I need to reconsider the user-agent string for HarmonyOS Next — it includes the Huawei Browser identifier but uses a WebKit rendering engine rather than Chromium, so for Playwright testing I should use the `webkit` browser engine with the proper HarmonyOS Next user-agent. Since this is a static Next.js blog with Tailwind CSS, the main rendering differences will be CSS-related, particularly around webkit-prefixed properties and flex/grid behavior. The current solution falls short because the Huawei Mate 60 profile incorrectly assumes Chromium when it actually ships with HarmonyOS Next's WebKit-based browser.

content.text:
**No, the current solution has a gap here.** Here's why:

### What HarmonyOS Next is

HarmonyOS Next (launched 2024, Mate 60 series and newer) is a **complete break from Android**:
- Own kernel — no Linux/AOSP base
- Default browser: **Huawei Browser**, which uses a **WebKit-based** rendering engine (not Chromium/Blink)
- Comes on: Mate 60, Pura 70, Mate X6, and newer Huawei flagships

### The gap in the current profiles

| Profile | What we test | Reality |
|---|---|---|
| Huawei P50 Pro | Chromium + Android UA | Correct — P50 runs EMUI/Android |
| **Huawei Mate 60** | **Chromium + Android UA** | **Wrong — Mate 60 ships HarmonyOS Next + WebKit browser** |

The Mate 60 profile is currently testing the wrong browser engine entirely.

### What should be added

A dedicated HarmonyOS Next profile using **WebKit** (to match Huawei Browser's rendering engine) with the correct UA string:

```ts
{
  name: 'Huawei Mate 60 (HarmonyOS Next)',
  use: {
    userAgent:
      'Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 12; HarmonyOS; BRA-AL00) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/122.0.0.0 HuaweiBrowser/15.0.6.301 Mobile Safari/537.36',
    viewport: { width: 393, height: 873 },
    deviceScaleFactor: 3,
    isMobile: true,
    hasTouch: true,
    defaultBrowserType: 'webkit',  // ← critical difference
  },
},
```

Also worth adding a **Pura 70 Pro** profile (top Huawei 2024 model, 393×873, also HarmonyOS Next).

For CSS correctness, since this is a Tailwind/Next.js static site, the most likely WebKit-specific issues would be `-webkit-` prefixed properties and any `backdrop-filter` behavior — which the navbar actually uses (`backdrop-blur-md`).
